message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Show Vulnerability Overview for VULN Role Users

## Context
The "Overview" menu item under Vulnerability Management in the sidebar (`/vulnerabilities/current`) is currently only visible to ADMIN and SECCHAMPION users. Users with the VULN role should also see it — they have access to the vulnerability management section but are missing the overview link.

**Image 8** (admin view): Shows "Overview" visible for `secmanadmin` (ADMIN role).
**Image 9** (regular user view): Shows "Overview" is missing for a VULN-only user — they only see System vulns, Outdated Assets, etc.

## File to Modify
- `src/frontend/src/components/Sidebar.tsx` (line ~254)

## Change

### Update the Overview visibility condition (line 254)

**Current:**
```tsx
{(userRoles.includes('ADMIN') || userRoles.includes('SECCHAMPION')) && (
```

**New:**
```tsx
{(userRoles.includes('ADMIN') || userRoles.includes('SECCHAMPION') || userRoles.includes('VULN')) && (
```

This adds `VULN` to the roles that can see the "Overview" link, matching the fact that VULN users already have backend access to `/api/vulnerabilities/current`.

**Note:** The comment above the line (`{/* Overview - ADMIN or SECCHAMPION only */}`) should also be updated to reflect the new condition.

## Verification
- `cd src/frontend && npm run build` — no build errors
- Log in as a user with VULN role (but not ADMIN/SECCHAMPION) → "Overview" should appear in the sidebar under Vulnerability Management


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/flake/.claude/projects/-Users-flake-sources-misc-secman/370e3f5e-7c1c-4014-8e1f-84ab3dd67362.jsonl

planContent:
# Plan: Show Vulnerability Overview for VULN Role Users

## Context
The "Overview" menu item under Vulnerability Management in the sidebar (`/vulnerabilities/current`) is currently only visible to ADMIN and SECCHAMPION users. Users with the VULN role should also see it — they have access to the vulnerability management section but are missing the overview link.

**Image 8** (admin view): Shows "Overview" visible for `secmanadmin` (ADMIN role).
**Image 9** (regular user view): Shows "Overview" is missing for a VULN-only user — they only see System vulns, Outdated Assets, etc.

## File to Modify
- `src/frontend/src/components/Sidebar.tsx` (line ~254)

## Change

### Update the Overview visibility condition (line 254)

**Current:**
```tsx
{(userRoles.includes('ADMIN') || userRoles.includes('SECCHAMPION')) && (
```

**New:**
```tsx
{(userRoles.includes('ADMIN') || userRoles.includes('SECCHAMPION') || userRoles.includes('VULN')) && (
```

This adds `VULN` to the roles that can see the "Overview" link, matching the fact that VULN users already have backend access to `/api/vulnerabilities/current`.

**Note:** The comment above the line (`{/* Overview - ADMIN or SECCHAMPION only */}`) should also be updated to reflect the new condition.

## Verification
- `cd src/frontend && npm run build` — no build errors
- Log in as a user with VULN role (but not ADMIN/SECCHAMPION) → "Overview" should appear in the sidebar under Vulnerability Management

content.text:
Build succeeds cleanly.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- The sidebar uses a **client-side RBAC pattern**: `userRoles` (parsed from the JWT) gates which menu items render. This is a UI-only guard — the backend `/api/vulnerabilities/current` endpoint already authorizes VULN users, so this change simply aligns the frontend visibility with existing backend permissions.
- The condition uses short-circuit `||` chaining — each `includes()` call is O(n) on the roles array, but since users typically have very few roles (1–3), this is negligible.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Done. The "Overview" link under Vulnerability Management is now visible to users with the `VULN` role, in addition to `ADMIN` and `SECCHAMPION`.
